title: Officina Stellare wins $2 million contract for lasercom ground station in Spain
summary: Officina Stellare secured a $2 million contract to build a laser communication ground station in Spain for ICFO. The station will support future space-to-Earth laser and quantum-encrypted communications.

title: Rocket Lab launches eighth Synspective radar imaging satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its eighth radar imaging satellite for Synspective, a Japanese company specializing in Earth observation. The mission marks another step in expanding Synspective's synthetic aperture radar (SAR) satellite constellation.

title: Another GPS launch shifts from ULA to SpaceX as Vulcan investigation continues
summary: A GPS satellite launch originally planned for ULA's Vulcan Centaur rocket has been reassigned to SpaceX due to ongoing investigations into the Vulcan rocket. The GPS III SV-10 satellite will now launch on a SpaceX vehicle.

title: OHB Sweden wins €248 million contract to build EPS-Sterna constellation
summary: OHB Sweden secured a €248 million contract to construct 20 satellites for the EPS-Sterna constellation, enhancing Europe's weather forecasting and climate monitoring. This deal marks the largest ever for Sweden's space sector.

title: Holistic space observation: the shift from SSA to SDA
summary: SpaceX's proposal to deploy up to one million satellites in low Earth orbit, combined with AI-enabled autonomous orbital infrastructure, signals a major shift toward large-scale, strategic space operations. This move reflects the industry's transition from Space Situational Awareness (SSA) to Space Domain Awareness (SDA).

title: ESA to fly dedicated Crew Dragon mission to ISS
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will charter a SpaceX Crew Dragon mission to the International Space Station (ISS) to provide more flight opportunities for its astronauts.

title: Kayhan targets investors, insurers with expanded orbital intelligence platform
summary: Kayhan Space is expanding its orbital intelligence platform to offer business insights for investors and insurers, moving beyond satellite fleet coordination. The new software aims to leverage existing data for broader financial and risk assessment applications.

title: Blue Origin joins the orbital data center race
summary: Blue Origin has entered the orbital data center market by filing plans for a constellation of up to 51,600 satellites. This move positions the company as a competitor in the emerging space-based data infrastructure sector.

title: Register now: The energy imperative driving the push toward orbital data centers
summary: A virtual event on March 31, sponsored by Star Catcher and the Commercial Space Federation, will discuss the energy imperative behind the push for orbital data centers. The event highlights the growing interest in space-based data infrastructure to address energy challenges.

title: Kratos wins $446 million Space Force contract for missile-tracking ground systems
summary: Kratos has secured a $446 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop ground management and integration systems for the Resilient Missile Warning and Tracking satellite program. This deal aims to enhance the military's missile-tracking capabilities.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 202 — Artemis Imminent
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' discusses the imminent Artemis missions with insights from Space.com's spaceflight and tech editor Mike Wall. The podcast covers the most recent updates on NASA's Artemis program.

title: Artemis Accords nations grapple with how to handle emergencies and 'harmful interference' on the moon
summary: Nations involved in the Artemis Accords are addressing challenges around emergency responses and preventing harmful interference during lunar missions. The discussions aim to manage growing competition and coordination issues on the moon.

title: Would you like to know more about 'Starship Troopers: Ultimate Bug War!'? Developer Auroch Digital has the answers (interview)
summary: Developer Auroch Digital discusses the latest Starship Troopers video game, highlighting its unique twists and strengths compared to previous versions. The interview explores the game's development and features.

title: Astronomers keep finding new moons of Jupiter and Saturn
summary: Astronomers have discovered four new moons orbiting Jupiter and 11 new moons around Saturn. These findings expand our understanding of the gas giants' satellite systems.

title: US Space Force moves GPS launch to SpaceX Falcon 9 due to Vulcan rocket glitch
summary: The U.S. Space Force has switched the launch provider for an upcoming GPS satellite from ULA's Vulcan Centaur to SpaceX's Falcon 9 due to an ongoing investigation into a rocket anomaly.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' is an optimistic look towards the stars, and we need that right now (review)
summary: The review of 'Project Hail Mary' highlights its optimistic portrayal of space exploration as a much-needed uplifting perspective. The article emphasizes the book's ability to inspire awe and wonder about the stars.

title: Hubble Space Telescope accidentally witnesses comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS) breaking apart
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a rare cosmic event as comet C/2025 K1 (ATLAS) broke apart in real time. This marks a significant observation for astronomers studying comet disintegration.

title: Northern lights may be visible in 18 states tonight and over the weekend
summary: Auroras may be visible in 18 U.S. states, including Alaska and Illinois, due to coronal mass ejections and solar wind impacting Earth. The Northern Lights could be seen over the weekend as a result of these solar events.

title: NASA’s X-59 Experimental Supersonic Aircraft Makes Second Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental supersonic aircraft completed its second flight, marking the start of numerous test flights planned for 2026. Despite an early landing due to a technical issue, the team gathered valuable data for future tests.

title: Hangar One Restoration Project
summary: Restoration of Hangar One, a historic aviation landmark in the San Francisco Bay Area, has been completed. The project was finished by Planetary Ventures in December 2025.

title: NASA Selects University Finalists for Technology Concepts Competition
summary: NASA selected 14 university teams as finalists for the 2026 RASC-AL Competition, challenging students to design innovative concepts for human life and work on the Moon, Mars, and beyond.

title: How Open NASA Data on Comet 3I/ATLAS Will Power Tomorrow’s Discoveries
summary: NASA's public data archives will preserve observations of interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS, captured by over a dozen missions as it exits the solar system. This marks only the third such object identified visiting our solar system from interstellar space.

title: Smiles and Spacesuits
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams undergoes a spacesuit fit verification inside the International Space Station's Quest airlock to ensure airtightness and mobility. The procedure is part of routine safety checks to prevent potential risks during spacewalks.

title: NASA Exploration, Science Inspire “Project Hail Mary” Film
summary: NASA's upcoming Artemis II mission, the first crewed flight under the Artemis program, inspires the sci-fi film 'Project Hail Mary,' premiering soon. The real-life space exploration milestone aligns with the fictional narrative, blending science fact and fiction.

title: NASA Simulations Improve Artemis II Launch Environment
summary: NASA used advanced simulations to optimize airflow around the Artemis II Space Launch System (SLS) rocket during its flight to the Moon, ensuring the best possible launch conditions for the test mission.

title: NASA Glenn Opens Applications for Free Summer Engineering Institute
summary: NASA Glenn Research Center is accepting applications for its free 2026 High School Engineering Institute, offering hands-on aerospace learning for rising juniors and seniors from March 20 to May 1. The program aims to prepare students for future aerospace careers through immersive NASA experiences and career readiness tools.

title: Restless Kīlauea Launches Lava and Ash
summary: Kīlauea volcano in Hawaii has entered its 43rd eruption phase, featuring high lava fountains and significant ash dispersal. This marks another active episode in the ongoing volcanic activity.

title: American Bald Eagle at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center in Florida conducted annual bald eagle nesting surveys on March 13, 2026. An American bald eagle was spotted flying away from its nest during the survey.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center. The company aims to enhance its manufacturing capabilities and expand into space-based data infrastructure.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the crewed lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship program ahead of Flight 12. This brings the next launch one step closer to readiness.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: Nine launches are scheduled for this week, featuring Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets. The launches highlight a busy period in the space industry.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects. The mission concept combines various technologies to address potential asteroid threats.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for human missions to the Moon and Mars at its Starfactory facility, transitioning from steel production to Starship development. The company's ambitious goals are highlighted in a recent update.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: The International Space Station (ISS) saw new crew arrivals and scientific activities in February after Crew-11 departed early due to a medical issue. The event highlights ongoing human spaceflight and research aboard the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket expected to dominate the launch schedule. The upcoming missions highlight the rocket's frequent deployment in a busy period for space launches.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a new phase after Flight 11 in October 2025. This follows a 147-day gap since the last mission.

title: Firefly Alpha FLTA007 “Stairway to Seven” launches successfully
summary: Firefly Aerospace successfully launched its Alpha FLTA007 rocket, marking its return to flight after a nearly 10-month pause. The mission, dubbed 'Stairway to Seven,' signifies a major milestone for the company's launch capabilities.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry-related topic, though the specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 years of its hallmark achievements. The publication highlights key milestones and contributions over the past five decades.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team confirmed the spacecraft is operational and 'phoned home'.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of industry events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25). These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, will monitor over 150,000 stars to detect Earth-like planets before its planned 2027 launch on Ariane 6.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will introduce ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, making it mandatory for all suppliers to use the new costing software in financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the root cause and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic systems and other technologies. The facility is part of ESA's efforts to support educational and research initiatives in space technology.

title: Kayhan Space Launches Satcat Terminal
summary: Kayhan Space has launched a new Satcat Terminal business, which may appeal to liberal arts majors. The initiative expands their offerings in the space industry.

title: Inside Austria’s Small But Mighty Space Ecosystem
summary: Austria's space industry is experiencing growth with increased funding and startup activity. The country's space ecosystem is described as small but mighty.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on a debris removal service to address operational, rather than experimental, needs. The initiative aims to maintain critical satellite infrastructure for communications, navigation, weather forecasting, and national security.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its SR75 suborbital rocket, targeting a launch no earlier than Q3 2026. This marks a significant step in HyImpulse's commercial launch capabilities.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first satellite bus contract in Japan with NEC Corporation, which will purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demo mission scheduled for next year.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket has undergone modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster margins, with the launch expected this summer. These changes were planned before a recent anomaly, and the company is accelerating their implementation.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round aimed at reducing reliance on satellite infrastructure. The investment highlights the growing interest in alternative navigation technologies within the space industry.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics secured a deal with South Korea's national security sector, marking another success in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights the growing collaboration between NanoAvionics and the South Korean space industry.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the transition period for Part 450 licensing requirements, with all US launchers now operating under the five-year-old regulations. This marks the end of legacy regulations for commercial space launches.

title: How Congress Can Grow the Space Supply Chain
summary: The space industry's supply chain is struggling to meet skyrocketing demand, but Congress can help accelerate growth according to industry groups.

title: Spire Targets 50% Growth in 2026 After Adjustments in 2025
summary: Spire Global aims for a 50% revenue growth in 2026 after restructuring its maritime business and reducing debt in 2025, as announced by CEO Theresa Condor.

title: Eutelsat CEO Fallacher Talks OneWeb Gen 2, Global Competition, and Sovereign Demand
summary: Eutelsat Group CEO Jean-François Fallacher discusses the company's transformation, OneWeb Gen 2 plans, global competition, and sovereign demand amid his nearly one-year tenure.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space Plan Repeatable Debris Removal Missions
summary: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space are collaborating to offer commercial debris removal missions under a recurring service model called Debris Removal as a Service (DRAAS). This partnership aims to provide regular, repeatable debris removal operations in space.
company: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space

title: SLI Purchases Two Small GEO Satellites From ReOrbit
summary: SLI has acquired two small Geostationary Orbit (GEO) communication satellites from ReOrbit for 150 million euros ($172.8 million). The deal was announced in a joint statement by both companies.

title: Comtech Delivers First Set of Digital Modems to US Army
summary: Comtech delivered the first set of Enterprise Digital Intermediate Frequency Multi-Carrier (EDIM) modems to the U.S. Army under a $48.6 million contract signed in 2023. The company announced the delivery in a recent press release.

title: Latest White House Cybersecurity Strategy Talks Crypto, AI, Quantum
summary: The White House released President Trump’s Cyber Strategy for America, focusing on securing critical infrastructure and advancing emerging technologies like crypto, AI, and quantum. The strategy outlines six policy pillars to maintain technological superiority.

title: Getting Better Outcomes In Space: How Will the UK Embrace the Challenge?
summary: A panel at Space-Comm in London discussed the security of UK space assets and strategic stability from both government and industry perspectives.

title: SpiderOak Wins New UAS Systems Contract
summary: SpiderOak has secured a new contract with the U.S. Army Unmanned Aircraft Systems (PMO UAS) to conduct supply chain analysis and cybersecurity assessments for multiple vendors. This contract highlights SpiderOak's role in enhancing security and reliability within the UAS ecosystem.

title: Ukraine Ambassador to UK Talks Future Warfare
summary: The Ukrainian ambassador to the U.K. discussed the future of warfare during an event at Chatham House, four years after Russia's invasion. Valerii Zaluzhnyi highlighted key aspects of modern conflict.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a proposal with the FCC to host an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, asserting that it will not cause interference. The company claims the SkyTerra Next proposal aligns with existing L-band coordination.
